        if kw.lower() in title:
            score += 4
    for kw in tiers["tier2"]:
        if kw.lower() in title:
            score += 2
    for kw in tiers["tier3"]:
        if kw.lower() in title:
            score += 1

    for dom, trust in DOMAIN_TRUST.items():
        if dom in domain:
            score += trust

    for level, words in NEGATIVE_KEYWORDS_SEVERITY.items():
        for kw in words:
            if kw.lower() in title:
                score += NEGATIVE_WEIGHTS[level]

    if any(c.isdigit() for c in title):
        score += 1

    if _contains_any(title, VAGUE_PATTERNS):
        score -= 3

    if _contains_any(title, CLICKBAIT_PATTERNS):
        score -= 4

    for phrase in phrases["strong_positive"]:
        if phrase.lower() in title:
            score += 6

    for phrase in phrases["strong_negative"]:
        if phrase.lower() in title:
            score -= 8

    score += _brand_match_score(title, subindustry)

    try:
        headline_emb = SEMANTIC_MODEL.encode(title, convert_to_tensor=True)
        sim = util.cos_sim(headline_emb, subindustry_embedding).item()
        score += sim * SEMANTIC_WEIGHT
    except Exception:
        sim = 0.0

    if sim < 0.25:
        score -= 8.0

    if pub_dt is not None:
        now = dt.datetime.utcnow().replace(tzinfo=pub_dt.tzinfo)
        delta_days = (now - pub_dt).total_seconds() / 86400.0
        score += max(-5.0, min(5.0, -delta_days * RECENCY_WEIGHT))

    return score, sim

def fetch(url: str, allow_redirects: bool = True) -> requests.Response:
    info(f"FETCH → {url}")
    r = requests.get(url, timeout=20, allow_redirects=allow_redirects, headers={"User-Agent": "Mozilla/5.0"})
    r.raise_for_status()
    return r

def parse_rss(content: str, url: str) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:
    items: List[Dict[str, Any]] = []
    try:
        root = ET.fromstring(content)
    except Exception as e:
        bad(f"RSS PARSE ERROR: {e}")
        return items

    for item in root.findall(".//item"):
        title = (item.findtext("title") or "").strip()
        link = (item.findtext("link") or "").strip()
        date = (item.findtext("pubDate") or "").strip()
        if title and link:
            items.append({"title": title, "link": link, "date": date or ""})

    if not items:
        ns = {"atom": "http://www.w3.org/2005/Atom"}
        for entry in root.findall(".//atom:entry", ns):
            title = (entry.findtext("atom:title", default="", namespaces=ns) or "").strip()
            link_el = entry.find("atom:link", ns)
            link = (link_el.get("href") if link_el is not None else "").strip()
            date = (entry.findtext("atom:updated", default="", namespaces=ns) or "").strip()
            if title and link:
                items.append({"title": title, "link": link, "date": date or ""})

    if items:
        good(f"RSS ITEMS: {len(items)}")
    else:
        info("No RSS items found")

    return items

def parse_webpage(content: str, url: str) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:
    soup = BeautifulSoup(content, "html.parser")
    title = soup.title.string.strip() if soup.title and soup.title.string else url
    good(f"WEB TITLE: {title}")
    return [{"title": title, "link": url, "date": ""}]

def collect_headlines_from_source(url: str) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:
    try:
        r = fetch(url)
    except Exception as e:
        bad(f"FETCH ERROR: {url} :: {e}")
        return []

    content = r.text
    lower = content.lower()
    if "<rss" in lower or "<feed" in lower:
        info("Detected RSS/Feed")
        items = parse_rss(content, url)
    else:
        info("Detected normal webpage")
        items = parse_webpage(content, url)

    for i, it in enumerate(items, start=1):
        info(f"  [{i}] {it['title']}")

    return items

def is_google_news_url(url: str) -> bool:
    parsed = urllib.parse.urlparse(url)
    return "news.google.com" in parsed.netloc

def resolve_google_news_redirect(url: str) -> str:
    section("GOOGLE NEWS RESOLVE")
    label("WRAPPER URL", url)
    try:
        r = fetch(url, allow_redirects=True)
        final_url = r.url
        if not is_google_news_url(final_url):
            good(f"RESOLVED → {final_url}")
            return final_url

        soup = BeautifulSoup(r.text, "lxml")
        a = soup.find("a", href=True)
        if a and a["href"]:
            real = a["href"]
            good(f"HTML LINK → {real}")
            return real

        bad("Could not resolve Google News wrapper, using original URL")
        return url
    except Exception as e:
        bad(f"RESOLVE ERROR: {e}")
        return url

def _extract_meta(soup: BeautifulSoup, names, props):
    for n in names:
        tag = soup.find("meta", attrs={"name": n})
        if tag and tag.get("content"):
            return tag["content"].strip()
    for p in props:
        tag = soup.find("meta", attrs={"property": p})
        if tag and tag.get("content"):
            return tag["content"].strip()
    return ""

def _extract_time(soup: BeautifulSoup):
    t = soup.find("time")
    if t and (t.get("datetime") or t.text.strip()):
        return (t.get("datetime") or t.text.strip())
    meta = _extract_meta(
        soup,
        ["article:published_time", "pubdate", "date", "dc.date"],
        ["article:published_time", "og:updated_time"],
    )
    return meta or "Unknown"

def _estimate_reading_time(text: str) -> str:
    words = len(text.split())
    minutes = max(1, int(round(words / 200.0)))
    return str(minutes)

def _quality_score(body_html: str) -> float:
    text = BeautifulSoup(body_html, "lxml").get_text(" ", strip=True)
    words = len(text.split())
    paragraphs = body_html.count("<p")
    score = 0.0
    if words > 300:
        score += 3
    elif words > 150:
        score += 2
    elif words > 60:
        score += 1
    if paragraphs > 5:
        score += 2
    elif paragraphs > 2:
        score += 1
    return score

def extract_full_article(url: str) -> Dict[str, Any]:
    section("ARTICLE EXTRACTION")
    label("ORIGINAL URL", url)

    if is_google_news_url(url):
        url = resolve_google_news_redirect(url)
    label("TARGET URL", url)

    html = None
    for attempt in range(3):
        try:
            r = fetch(url, allow_redirects=True)
            html = r.text
            break
        except Exception as e:
            bad(f"ARTICLE FETCH ERROR (attempt {attempt+1}/3): {e}")
    if html is None:
        return {
            "title": "",
            "dek": "",
            "body_html": "",
            "hero_image": "",
            "hero_caption": "",
            "published_at": "Unknown",
            "updated_at": "Unknown",
            "reading_time": "3",
            "quality_score": 0.0,
        }

    soup = BeautifulSoup(html, "lxml")

    body_html = ""
    dek = ""
    readable_title = ""

    try:
        downloaded = trafilatura.extract(html, include_comments=False, include_tables=False, output_format="xml")
        if downloaded:
            tsoup = BeautifulSoup(downloaded, "lxml")
            parts = []
            for el in tsoup.find_all(["p", "h2", "h3", "ul", "ol", "li", "blockquote"]):
                parts.append(str(el))
            body_html = "\n".join(parts).strip()
            good("Trafilatura extraction OK")
    except Exception as e:
        bad(f"Trafilatura failed: {e}")

    if not body_html:
        try:
            doc = Document(html)
            readable_title = (doc.short_title() or "").strip()
            readable_html = doc.summary()
            readable_soup = BeautifulSoup(readable_html, "lxml")

            body_parts = []
            for el in readable_soup.find_all(["p", "h2", "h3", "ul", "ol", "li", "blockquote"]):
                body_parts.append(str(el))
            body_html = "\n".join(body_parts).strip()

            first_p = readable_soup.find("p")
            dek = first_p.get_text(strip=True) if first_p else ""

            good("Readability extraction OK")
        except Exception as e:
            bad(f"Readability failed: {e}")

    if not body_html:
        try:
            art = NPArticle(url)
            art.download()
            art.parse()
            text = art.text
            body_html = "\n".join(f"<p>{p.strip()}</p>" for p in text.split("\n") if p.strip())
            if not readable_title:
                readable_title = art.title
            good("newspaper3k extraction OK")
        except Exception as e:
            bad(f"newspaper3k failed: {e}")

    if not body_html:
        article_tag = soup.find("article")
        if article_tag:
            parts = []
            for el in article_tag.find_all(["p", "h2", "h3", "ul", "ol", "li", "blockquote"]):
                parts.append(str(el))
            body_html = "\n".join(parts).strip()
            good("Fallback: <article> content used")
        else:
            parts = []
            for el in soup.find_all("p"):
                txt = el.get_text(strip=True)
                if len(txt) > 40:
                    parts.append(f"<p>{txt}</p>")
            body_html = "\n".join(parts).strip()
            good("Fallback: <p> content used")

    page_title = readable_title or (soup.title.string.strip() if soup.title and soup.title.string else url)

    if not dek:
        dek = _extract_meta(
            soup,
            ["description", "twitter:description"],
            ["og:description"],
        )

    hero_image = _extract_meta(
        soup,
        ["twitter:image", "image"],
        ["og:image", "og:image:url"],
    )
    hero_caption = page_title

    published_at = _extract_time(soup)
    updated_at = published_at

    plain_text = BeautifulSoup(body_html or "", "lxml").get_text(" ", strip=True)
    reading_time = _estimate_reading_time(plain_text) if plain_text else "1"

    q_score = _quality_score(body_html or "")

    label("EXTRACTED TITLE", page_title)
    label("READING TIME", f"{reading_time} min")
    label("QUALITY SCORE", f"{q_score:.2f}")

    return {
        "title": page_title,
        "dek": dek or page_title,
        "body_html": body_html or f"<p>{page_title}</p>",
        "hero_image": hero_image,
        "hero_caption": hero_caption,
        "published_at": published_at,
        "updated_at": updated_at,
        "reading_time": reading_time,
        "quality_score": q_score,
    }

def _sanitize_for_json(obj):
    if isinstance(obj, dict):
        clean = {}
        for k, v in obj.items():
            if isinstance(v, (dt.datetime, dt.date)):
                clean[k] = v.isoformat()
            else:
                clean[k] = _sanitize_for_json(v)
        return clean
    elif isinstance(obj, list):
        return [_sanitize_for_json(i) for i in obj]
    else:
        return obj

def build_source_links_html(items: List[Dict[str, Any]]) -> str:

        t = item["title"]
        l = item["link"]
        lines.append(
            '            <li>'

def build_article_body_html(article_data: Dict[str, Any], backlinks: List[Dict[str, Any]]) -> str:
    parts = []
    parts.append(article_data["body_html"])
    if backlinks:
        parts.append("<h2>Additional Coverage</h2>")
        parts.append("<ul>")
        for item in backlinks:
            t = item["title"]
            l = item["link"]
            parts.append(f'<li><a href="{l}" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">{t}</a></li>')
        parts.append("</ul>")
    return "\n".join(parts)

def compute_density_level(items: List[Dict[str, Any]]) -> Tuple[str, str]:
    now = dt.datetime.utcnow()
    recent = 0
    for it in items:
        pub_dt = it.get("pub_dt")
        if isinstance(pub_dt, dt.datetime):
            delta_days = (now - pub_dt.replace(tzinfo=None)).total_seconds() / 86400.0
            if delta_days <= 3:
                recent += 1
    if recent >= 15:
        return "extreme", "Extreme signal: intense coverage and rapid developments."
    elif recent >= 8:
        return "high", "High signal: elevated coverage and strong momentum."
    elif recent >= 3:
        return "medium", "Normal signal: steady flow of relevant coverage."
    else:
        return "low", "Low signal: light coverage, industry is relatively quiet."

def kernel_source_expand(base_sources: List[str]) -> List[str]:
    expanded = set()
    for src in base_sources:
        expanded.add(src)
        try:
            parsed = urllib.parse.urlparse(src)
            root = f"{parsed.scheme}://{parsed.netloc}"
            expanded.add(root)
            for path in ["/news", "/business", "/markets", "/press", "/stories"]:
                expanded.add(root + path)
        except Exception:
            continue
    return list(expanded)

def kernel_news_discovery(gics: dict, limit: int = 10) -> List[str]:
    sub = gics["subindustry"].replace("-", " ")
    industry = gics["industry"].replace("-", " ")
    queries = [
        f"{sub} news",
        f"{sub} earnings",
        f"{sub} store closures",
        f"{industry} news",
        f"{industry} outlook",
    ]
    urls = set()
    try:
        g = GNews(language="en", country="US")
        for q in queries:
            arts = g.get_news(q)
            for a in arts[:limit]:
                link = a.get("url")
                if link:
                    urls.add(link)
    except Exception as e:
        bad(f"GNews kernel error: {e}")
    try:
        for q in queries:
            for link in google_search(q, num_results=limit):
                urls.add(link)
    except Exception as e:
        bad(f"Google search kernel error: {e}")
    return list(urls)

def kernel_semantic_filter(urls: List[str], gics: dict, threshold: float = 0.25) -> List[str]:
    prompt = _subindustry_semantic_prompt(gics)
    prompt_emb = SEMANTIC_MODEL.encode(prompt, convert_to_tensor=True)
    filtered = []
    for u in urls:
        try:
            title = u
            emb = SEMANTIC_MODEL.encode(title, convert_to_tensor=True)
            sim = util.cos_sim(emb, prompt_emb).item()
            if sim >= threshold:
                filtered.append(u)
        except Exception:
            continue
    return filtered

def kernel_backlink_discovery(article_html: str, base_url: str) -> List[str]:
    soup = BeautifulSoup(article_html, "lxml")
    links = set()
    for a in soup.find_all("a", href=True):
        href = a["href"]
        if href.startswith("#"):
            continue
        if href.startswith("/"):
            try:
                parsed = urllib.parse.urlparse(base_url)
                href = f"{parsed.scheme}://{parsed.netloc}{href}"
            except Exception:
                continue
        if href.startswith("http"):
            links.add(href)
    return list(links)

def kernel_density(items: List[Dict[str, Any]]) -> Tuple[str, str]:
    return compute_density_level(items)

def deduplicate_items(items: List[Dict[str, Any]]) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:
    if not items:
        return items
    section("DEDUPLICATION")
    titles = [it["title"].lower() for it in items]
    embeddings = SEMANTIC_MODEL.encode(titles, convert_to_tensor=True)
    keep_indices = []
    used = set()
    for i in range(len(items)):
        if i in used:
            continue
        keep_indices.append(i)
        for j in range(i + 1, len(items)):

            sim = util.cos_sim(embeddings[i], embeddings[j]).item()
            if sim > 0.85:
                used.add(j)
    deduped = [items[i] for i in keep_indices]
    good(f"DEDUPED: {len(items)} → {len(deduped)}")
    return deduped

def derive_page_name(path):

def now_utc():
    return dt.datetime.now(dt.timezone.utc)

def normalize_dt(dt_obj):
    if not isinstance(dt_obj, dt.datetime):
        return None
    if dt_obj.tzinfo is None:
        return dt_obj.replace(tzinfo=dt.timezone.utc)
    return dt_obj.astimezone(dt.timezone.utc)

def within_last_days(dt_obj, days=7):
    dt_norm = normalize_dt(dt_obj)
    if not dt_norm:
        return False
    return dt_norm >= now_utc() - dt.timedelta(days=days)

def clean_url(raw):
    if not raw:
        return None
    url = "".join(ch for ch in raw if ch.isprintable())
    url = url.strip()
    if " " in url:
        return None
    if not re.match(r"^https?://[A-Za-z0-9\.-]+\.[A-Za-z]{2,}(/.*)?$", url):
        return None
    return url

def is_forbidden_fragment(url):
    bad_fragments = [
        "Sources for industry",
        "source.txt",
        "index.html",
        "/cards/",
        "/articles/",
    ]
    lower = url.lower()
    return any(bad.lower() in lower for bad in bad_fragments)

import re

def fetch_ddgs_text(console, topic):

def extract_date_from_url(url):
    m = re.search(r"/(20[0-9]{2})/([01][0-9])/([0-3][0-9])/", url)
    if m:
        try:
            y, mth, d = int(m.group(1)), int(m.group(2)), int(m.group(3))
            return dt.datetime(y, mth, d, tzinfo=dt.timezone.utc)
        except Exception:
            return None
    return None

def extract_date_from_article(url):

def extract_date_from_feed_entry(entry):
    for key in ["published", "updated", "created"]:
        if key in entry:
            try:
                return dateutil_parser.parse(entry[key])
            except Exception:
                continue
    return None

def discover_feeds_for_domain(console, url):

    feeds = []

    FEED_CACHE[domain] = feeds

def feed_is_recent(console, feed_url, days=7):
    if feed_url in FEED_RECENT_CACHE:
        return FEED_RECENT_CACHE[feed_url]
    try:
        parsed = feedparser.parse(feed_url)
        for entry in parsed.entries[:10]:
            dt_obj = extract_date_from_feed_entry(entry)
            if dt_obj and within_last_days(dt_obj, days=days):
                FEED_RECENT_CACHE[feed_url] = True

    except Exception:
        pass
    FEED_RECENT_CACHE[feed_url] = False

def is_live_and_recent_fast(console, url):

        if head.status_code >= 400:
            LIVE_RECENT_CACHE[url] = False
            return False
    except Exception:
        LIVE_RECENT_CACHE[url] = False
        return False
    feeds = discover_feeds_for_domain(console, url)
    for f in feeds:
        if feed_is_recent(console, f, days=7):
            LIVE_RECENT_CACHE[url] = True
            return True
    LIVE_RECENT_CACHE[url] = True
    return True

def tokenize(text):
    return re.findall(r"[A-Za-z0-9]+", text.lower())

def build_industry_keywords(topic):
    base = tokenize(topic)
    extra = []
    for token in base:
        if token.endswith("s"):
            extra.append(token[:-1])
        else:
            extra.append(token + "s")
    return set(base + extra)

def keyword_score(text, keywords):
    toks = tokenize(text)
    if not toks:
        return 0.0
    hits = sum(1 for t in toks if t in keywords)
    return hits / len(toks)

def score_source(console, url, topic_keywords):
    score = 0.0
    if is_news_like_path(url):
        score += 1.5
    dt_url = extract_date_from_url(url)
    if dt_url and within_last_days(dt_url, days=30):
        score += 2.0
    try:
        art = NewsPlease.from_url(url)
        if art:
            title = art.title or ""
            body = art.maintext or ""
            ks_title = keyword_score(title, topic_keywords)
            ks_body = keyword_score(body, topic_keywords)
            score += ks_title * 4.0
            score += ks_body * 2.0
            if art.date_publish and within_last_days(art.date_publish, days=30):
                score += 2.0
    except Exception:
        pass
    domain = normalize_domain(url)
    rep = DOMAIN_REPUTATION.get(domain, 0.0)
    score += rep
    return score

def update_domain_reputation(url, delta):

def cluster_candidate_titles(console, candidates):
    if not candidates:
        return
    titles = [c["title"] for c in candidates]
    vectorizer = TfidfVectorizer(stop_words="english")
    X = vectorizer.fit_transform(titles)
    k = min(5, len(titles))
    if k <= 1:
        return
    km = KMeans(n_clusters=k, random_state=42, n_init=10)
    labels = km.fit_predict(X)
    clusters = {}
    for idx, lbl in enumerate(labels):
        clusters.setdefault(lbl, []).append(candidates[idx])
    table = Table(title="Source Theme Clusters")
    table.add_column("Cluster")
    table.add_column("Top Titles")
    for cid, items in clusters.items():
        top_titles = [it["title"][:80] for it in items[:3]]
        table.add_row(str(cid), "\n".join(top_titles))
    console.print(table)

def discover_sources(console, topic, folder_path):
    console.print(Panel(f"Discovering sources for: [bold]{topic}[/bold]", title="Source Discovery", border_style="blue"))

    existing_sources = collect_all_existing_sources(folder_path)
    existing_set = set(existing_sources)

    topic_keywords = build_industry_keywords(topic)

    ddgs_urls = discover_sources_ddgs(console, topic, max_results=30)
    google_urls = discover_sources_google(console, topic, max_results=30)

    candidate_urls = []
    for u in ddgs_urls + google_urls:
        cu = clean_url(u)
        if not cu:
            continue
        if is_forbidden_fragment(cu):

        candidate_urls.append(cu)

    sitemap_urls = []
    for u in candidate_urls[:10]:
        sitemap_urls.extend(discover_from_sitemaps(console, u, max_urls=20))

    all_candidates = list(set(candidate_urls + sitemap_urls))
    console.print(f"[info]Total raw candidates: {len(all_candidates)}[/info]")

    fast_pass = []

        if len(fast_pass) >= FAST_PASS_MAX_HEADS_PER_PAGE:
            break
        if not is_live_and_recent_fast(console, u):

        fast_pass.append(u)

    console.print(f"[info]Fast-pass survivors: {len(fast_pass)}[/info]")

    scored = []

    scored.sort(key=lambda x: x["score"], reverse=True)

    new_sources = []
    all_pip_names = set()

        u = c["url"]
        if u in existing_set:
            continue
        if len(new_sources) >= MAX_NEW_SOURCES_PER_PAGE:
            break
        domain = normalize_domain(u)
        if sum(1 for s in existing_sources + new_sources if normalize_domain(s) == domain) >= MAX_PER_DOMAIN:
            continue
        new_sources.append(u)
        update_domain_reputation(u, 0.5)

    all_sources = existing_sources + new_sources
    if len(all_sources) > MAX_SOURCES_PER_FOLDER:
        all_sources = all_sources[:MAX_SOURCES_PER_FOLDER]

    source_file = os.path.join(folder_path, "source.txt")

        ))

# ---------------------------------------------------------
# Build the 5 fineprint blocks
# ---------------------------------------------------------

def build_blocks(topic, ddgs_text, wiki_text):
    base = (wiki_text + " " + ddgs_text).replace("\n", " ").strip()
    if not base:
        base = f"{topic} is a global classification standard."

    def summarize(text, length=300):
        text = re.sub(r'\s+', ' ', text).strip()
        if len(text) <= length:
            return text
        cut = text[:length]
        last_period = cut.rfind(".")
        if last_period != -1:
            return cut[:last_period+1]
        return cut.rstrip() + "…"

    summary1 = summarize(base)
    summary2 = summarize(base[len(base)//3:])
    summary3 = summarize(base[(len(base)//3)*2:])

    return {
        "Industry Definition":
            f"{topic} refers to {summary1}",

        "Why This Industry Matters":
            f"This classification matters because {summary2}",

        "Sector Context":
            f"Within the broader economic context, {summary3}",

        "Coverage Scope":
            f"Coverage includes sectors, industry groups, industries, and sub‑industries defined under {topic}.",

        "Data & Sources":
            DATA_SOURCES_TEXT
    }

# ---------------------------------------------------------
# Replace ONLY the micro-body text
# ---------------------------------------------------------

def replace_block(console, html, label, new_text):
    pattern = rf'(?is)(<div class="micro-label">\s*{re.escape(label)}\s*</div>\s*<div class="micro-body">)(.*?)(</div>)'
    repl = rf'\1{new_text}\3'
    new_html, count = re.subn(pattern, repl, html, count=1)
    preview = (new_text[:240] + "…") if len(new_text) > 240 else new_text
    if count == 0:
        console.print(f"[yellow][WARN][/yellow] Label not found: [white]{label}[/white]")
    else:
        console.print(Panel(preview or "(empty)", title=f"Updated Block: {label}", border_style="green"))
    return new_html

# ---------------------------------------------------------
# LETTER‑ONLY SANITATION
# ---------------------------------------------------------

def sanitize_labels(html, correct_name):

    html, _ = re.subn(
        r'(<h1>)(.*?)(</h1>)',
        rf'\1{correct_name}\3',
        html,
        flags=re.DOTALL
    )

    html, _ = re.subn(
        r'(<div class="signal-title">)(.*?)(</div>)',
        rf'\1{correct_name} · Live Industry Signal\3',
        html,
        flags=re.DOTALL
    )

    html, _ = re.subn(
        r'(>)(.*?)(\s*·\s*LIVE TERMINAL<)',
        rf'\1{correct_name} · LIVE TERMINAL<',
        html
    )

    html, _ = re.subn(
        r'(Banking With Billy)\s*·\s*\.\s*(Sector Intelligence)',
        rf'\1 · {correct_name} · \2',
        html,
        flags=re.IGNORECASE
    )

    return html

# ---------------------------------------------------------
# UPGRADE DETECTION
# ---------------------------------------------------------

def is_already_upgraded(html, topic):
    if UPGRADE_MARKER in html:
        return True
    if DATA_SOURCES_TEXT in html:
        return True
    if (f"{topic} refers to" in html and
        "This classification matters because" in html and
        "Within the broader economic context," in html):
        return True
    return False

# ---------------------------------------------------------
# PROCESS SINGLE PAGE
# ---------------------------------------------------------

def process_single_page(console, page_path):
    folder = os.path.dirname(page_path)
    topic = derive_page_name(page_path)

    console.print(Panel(
        f"[white]{page_path}[/white]\nDerived Name: [bold]{topic}[/bold]",
        title="Cleaning Page",
        border_style="blue"
    ))

    with open(page_path, "r", encoding="utf-8", errors="ignore") as f:
        html = f.read()

    already_upgraded = is_already_upgraded(html, topic)

    if already_upgraded:
        console.print("[cyan]Page already upgraded — skipping fineprint rebuild, running light sanitation only.[/cyan]")
        html = sanitize_labels(html, topic)
    else:
        ddgs_text = fetch_ddgs_text(console, topic)
        wiki_text = fetch_wikipedia_text(console, topic)
        blocks = build_blocks(topic, ddgs_text, wiki_text)

        labels = [
            "Industry Definition",
            "Why This Industry Matters",
            "Sector Context",
            "Coverage Scope",
            "Data & Sources"
        ]

        for label_name in labels:
            html = replace_block(console, html, label_name, blocks[label_name])

        html = sanitize_labels(html, topic)
        if UPGRADE_MARKER not in html:
            html += "\n" + UPGRADE_MARKER + "\n"

    with open(page_path, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
        f.write(html)

    discover_sources(console, topic, folder)

    console.print(Panel("[bold green]Page cleaned and sources rotated/updated.[/bold green]", border_style="green"))

# ---------------------------------------------------------
# WALK ENTIRE GICS TREE (SKIP 'cards' AND 'articles')
# ---------------------------------------------------------

def process_gics_tree():
    console = Console()

    console.print(Panel(
        f"Scanning GICS tree under:\n[white]{GICS_ROOT}[/white]",
        title="GICS Tree Cleaner + Source Engine (Pulse Substrate)",
        border_style="blue"
    ))

    for root, dirs, files in os.walk(GICS_ROOT):

        dirs[:] = [
            d for d in dirs
            if d.lower() not in ("cards", "articles")
        ]

        if "cards" in root.lower() or "articles" in root.lower():
            continue

        if "index.html" in files:
            page_path = os.path.join(root, "index.html")
            process_single_page(console, page_path)

# ============================================================
# RUNNERS
# ============================================================

def run_pulse_news_engine():
    banner("PULSE · NEWS ENGINE · BANKING WITH BILLY")
    for root, dirs, files in os.walk(GICS_ROOT):
        if os.path.basename(root).lower() == "articles":
            process_articles_folder(root)

def run_pulse_substrate():
    process_gics_tree()

def main():
    print("[PULSE] Starting full Pulse cycle…")
    print("[PULSE] Phase 1 — News Engine")
    run_pulse_news_engine()
    print("[PULSE] Phase 2 — Substrate (GICS cleaner + source engine)")
    run_pulse_substrate()
    print("[PULSE] Cycle complete.")

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

SCRIPTS_DIR = ROOT
LAYERS_DIR = os.path.join(ROOT, "layers")
LAYER_DIR_L1 = os.path.join(LAYERS_DIR, "layer1")
LAYER_DIR_L2 = os.path.join(LAYERS_DIR, "layer2")
LAYER_DIR_L3 = os.path.join(LAYERS_DIR, "layer3")
LAYER_DIR_L4 = os.path.join(LAYERS_DIR, "layer4")
LAYER0_JSON_PATH = os.path.join(LAYERS_DIR, "layer0_genesis_master.json")


def ensure_dirs():
    os.makedirs(LAYERS_DIR, exist_ok=True)


def read_text_file(path):
    if not os.path.exists(path):
        return None
    with open(path, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
        return f.read()


def read_json_file(path):
    if not os.path.exists(path):
        return None
    with open(path, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
        return json.load(f)


def collect_layer_json():
    """
    Collects all known layer JSONs (1–4, and any others you add later).
    Adjust filenames as your real files evolve.
    """
    layer_data = {}

    # If you already have a combined 1–3 JSON, plug it here
    # Example: layer3_pulse_substrate.json in scripts root
    layer1_3_combined = read_json_file(os.path.join(SCRIPTS_DIR, "layer3_pulse_substrate.json"))
    if layer1_3_combined is not None:
        layer_data["layer_1_3_combined"] = layer1_3_combined

    # Layer 4 business skeletons
    layer4 = read_json_file(os.path.join(SCRIPTS_DIR, "layer4_business_skeletons.json"))
    if layer4 is not None:
        layer_data["layer_4_business_skeletons"] = layer4

    # Optional: if you later write separate layer JSONs into /layers/
    for name, folder in [
        ("layer_1_substrate", LAYER_DIR_L1),
        ("layer_2_immortal_physics", LAYER_DIR_L2),
        ("layer_3_civilization", LAYER_DIR_L3),
        ("layer_4_business_skeletons_alt", LAYER_DIR_L4),
    ]:
        json_path = os.path.join(folder, f"{name}.json")
        data = read_json_file(json_path)
        if data is not None:
            layer_data[name] = data

    return layer_data


def collect_script_sources():
    """
    Packs your Python source files into the master JSON so the world
    can resurrect itself on any machine.
    Add/remove filenames as your studio grows.
    """
    scripts = {}

    candidate_files = [
        "foldercleaner.py",
        "pulsenews.py",
        "dna.py",
        "dnamaker.py",
        "layer3_civilization_dna.py",
        "layer4_business_skeletons_dna.py",
        "executor_layer5.py",
        "layer0_genesis_creator.py",  # this file itself, once you rename it
    ]

    for filename in candidate_files:
        path = os.path.join(SCRIPTS_DIR, filename)
        code = read_text_file(path)
        if code is not None:
            scripts[filename] = {
                "language": "python",
                "path_hint": f"./scripts/{filename}",
                "code": code,
            }

    return scripts


def build_resurrection_instructions():
    """
    High-level instructions for how to rebuild the world from this JSON.
    This is the human + machine readable resurrection protocol.
    """
    return {
        "description": "How to resurrect Pulse on any machine from this Layer 0 master file.",
        "steps": [
            "1. Create a root folder (e.g., breaking-news/).",
            "2. Create a scripts/ folder and a layers/ folder inside it.",
            "3. Read this layer0_genesis_master.json file.",
            "4. Recreate all Python files from master_json['scripts'][*]['code'] into ./scripts/.",
            "5. Recreate all layer JSON files from master_json['layers'][*] into ./layers/ or ./scripts/ as needed.",
            "6. Run the DNA creators (Layer 1–4) to regenerate any derived structures.",
            "7. Run the executor engines (Layer 5) to bring the civilization online.",
            "8. Optionally run foldercleaner / resurrection scripts to heal and verify the file universe."
        ],
        "notes": [
            "This file is the immortal blueprint. As long as it exists, Pulse can be rebuilt.",
            "You can extend this protocol with more precise folder maps and HTML template writers."
        ]
    }


def build_layer0_genesis_master():
    now = datetime.utcnow().isoformat() + "Z"

    master = {
        "layer": 0,
        "name": "Genesis Creator",
        "version": "1.0.0-layer0",
        "generated_at_utc": now,
        "description": "Master DNA file containing Layers 1–4 and all core Python source code for resurrection.",
        "world_metadata": {
            "author": "Billy Banks",
            "engine": "Pulse",
            "location_hint": "breaking-news/",
            "notes": "This file is designed to be portable across machines and environments."
        },
        "layers": collect_layer_json(),
        "scripts": collect_script_sources(),
        "resurrection_protocol": build_resurrection_instructions(),
    }

    return master


def write_layer0_file():
    ensure_dirs()
    master = build_layer0_genesis_master()
    with open(LAYER0_JSON_PATH, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
        json.dump(master, f, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False)
    print("Layer 0 (Genesis Master) written to:")
    print(" -", LAYER0_JSON_PATH)
    print()
    print("Included sections:")
    print(" - layers:", list(master["layers"].keys()))
    print(" - scripts:", list(master["scripts"].keys()))


def main():
    write_layer0_file()


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
